hooks.command: input=$(cat); printf '%s' "$input" | grep -qE '"file_path"[^,}]*\.py' || exit 0; uv run ruff format . >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

input=$(cat); printf '%s' "$input" | grep -q 'gh pr create' || exit 0; gate(){ if ! out=$(eval "$1" 2>&1); then { echo "PR blocked: $2 failed. Fix it, then retry gh pr create."; echo "$out"; } >&2; exit 2; fi; }; gate 'uv run ruff check .' 'ruff lint'; gate 'uv run ruff format --check .' 'ruff format-check'; gate 'uv run pyright' 'pyright'; gate 'uv run bandit -c pyproject.toml -r src/ -q' 'bandit'; gate 'uv run pip-audit || uv run pip-audit --vulnerability-service osv' 'pip-audit'; gate 'uv run pytest --cov --cov-fail-under=90' 'pytest (coverage>=90%)'

echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"SessionStart","additionalContext":"Skill-authoring policy (this project): when you finish a task and notice a repeatable, generalizable workflow that is likely to recur here and passes the substitution test (still meaningful after removing project-specific nouns), proactively create a project skill at .claude/skills/<kebab-name>/SKILL.md (frontmatter: name + description, plus a clear when-to-use), then tell the user what you created and why. Do this only when the benefit is real; never for one-off or trivial work. Prefer extending an existing skill over creating a near-duplicate. Complements the PLAYBOOK-marker convention in AGENTS.md."}}'
